items.question: How long does it take to build a website?
items.answer: The timeline depends on complexity. A basic website usually takes 1-2 weeks, while medium-scale systems may take more than a month.

items.question: What do I need to prepare?
items.answer: Prepare company profile text, services or product lists, and any images you want to show. If you do not have them yet, our team can help guide you.

items.question: Do you provide maintenance after launch?
items.answer: Yes. We offer monthly or annual maintenance packages covering updates, backups, monitoring, and ongoing fixes.

items.question: Can you repair my existing website?
items.answer: Yes. We audit your website first, identify issues such as errors, speed, or layout problems, and recommend the best fix.

items.question: How does payment work?
items.answer: Payment is usually made with a 30% deposit and the remaining balance after the website is completed and approved.

schema.about: Terms for using Codeverta software development services

terms.title: Terms & Conditions
terms.intro: Welcome to Codeverta & BikinWebsiteJogja. By accessing our site or using our services, you agree to the following terms.

services.title: 1. Scope of Services
services.content: Codeverta provides software development, website creation, application development, system integration, and IT consulting according to the approved proposal or statement of work.

payment.title: 2. Payment Terms
payment.content: Payments follow the agreed proposal, invoice, or milestone schedule. A deposit is required before production begins, and final payment is due before final asset handover.

ip.title: 3. Intellectual Property
ip.content: After full payment, source code usage rights or ownership are granted according to the contract. Third-party libraries, templates, licences, and core frameworks remain subject to their own licences.

liability.title: 4. Limitation of Liability
liability.content: We are not liable for losses caused by system misuse, changes by other parties, hosting/domain issues, or third-party integrations after the warranty or maintenance period ends.

cta.title: Still have another question?

items.question: How long does development take for each type of system?
items.answer: The timeline depends on project scale and complexity. A simple website often takes 1-2 weeks, mid-sized systems such as E-commerce, POS, and HRMS take about 1-3 months, while ERP or WMS can take several months.

items.question: What should I prepare before the project starts?
items.answer: Prepare feature requirements, design references, initial data structure, content, photos, and company documents. For technical projects, we help refine requirements and business workflows before development.

items.question: Can the system be expanded in the future?
items.answer: Yes. Systems such as E-commerce, ERP, HRMS, CRM, and WMS are built modularly so new features, API integrations, payments, or server migration can be added in stages.

items.question: Do you provide maintenance after launch?
items.answer: Yes. We offer monthly or annual maintenance covering bug fixes, security updates, server monitoring, backups, and small feature additions.

items.question: Can you build bespoke features for my business?
items.answer: Yes. We can customise modules, workflows, reports, and integrations such as Midtrans, GeoServer, Socket.io, GraphQL, Kafka, RFID, or other systems.

items.question: What is the workflow from order to deployment?
items.answer: The process starts with consultation, requirement analysis, proposal, initial payment, UI/UX design, development, testing, revision, finalisation, final payment, and deployment to your server or cloud.

items.question: How does payment work for software development?
items.answer: Payment usually starts with a 30% deposit and the remaining balance after the project is completed and approved. Larger projects can be paid by milestone.

items.question: What technologies do you use?
items.answer: The stack depends on the project. We use technologies such as Next.js, React, Vue, Laravel, Node.js, Django, PostgreSQL, Redis, Docker, Kafka, PostGIS, and cloud services.

items.question: Are the systems responsive on different devices?
items.answer: Yes. Our web systems are responsive on desktop, tablet, and smartphone. POS or WMS interfaces can also be optimised for touch devices and field operations.
